title: Angkor Wat—A Transcultural History of Heritage Volume 1: Angkor in France. From Plaster Casts to Exhibition Pavilions. Volume 2: Angkor in Cambodia. From Jungle Find to Global Icon, by Michael Falser
highlights: * Falser exposes how and why Mouhot, by the 1860s, formulated his "architectural hymn" for Angkor through a unique blend of French colonialist and missionary rhetoric.  ...    monograph discusses the multiple lives of Angkor Wat over a 150-year-long period from the 1860s to the 2010s. title: Stop saying the French discovered Angkor | Alison in Cambodia
highlights: of my biggest pet peeves is the old myth about how the French, specifically the explorer and researcher Henri Mouhot, “discovered Angkor” in 1860. This myth is based on an idea that the  Cambodians had no knowledge of their past, and therefore helped the French justify their colonial rule in “restoring a nation to its past grandeur” (Dagens 1995:47).  As Angkor has been in the news lately,due to the recent BBC documentary, this factual inaccuracy continues to  ...    these images that captured the imagination of the West.  Mouhot never claimed to have discovered Angkor, this topic was  ...    During the French Colonial period the Angkor Wat and the Angkorian past became an integral part of the Cambodian nation  ...    remained, witha few brief exceptions, ever since. While the French Protectorate certainly influenced our perceptions and understanding of Cambodian history, it is important to acknowledge that they did not discover Angkor. Many Cambodians were already aware of Angkor Wat
